review_text: I got this blade in the mail a few years ago, Gillette sent this out to potential customers. I tried it and it's a decent blade. They use 3 blades, but while it is better than single or double blades, the swivel effect from the razor itself makes getting cut by the blades rather easy. I got a cut at least 2 times a month. It would leave me a bit irritated too. The price for the blades were a bit much too. The strips wore down rather quickly, but I've moved onto the Schick Quattro and haven't looked back since.

review_text: Although these blades are marketed for men, I am a woman and love them more than the Venus "women's version". If you like the Venus handle though these blades will clip right on, I love it. And talk about long lasting- I kid you not: I shave my armpits every time I shower- almost everyday, and it is sick but I have used the same blade for weeks and weeks! I don't recommend that, but you've gotta do what you've gotta do. Mach3 may be on the expensive side but they last longer and work better (very smooth shave) than any blade I've tried.

review_text: Mach3 is an enormous improvement over the Sensor, don't get me wrong. It's still not great, though. I always thought that safety razors were supposed to be, well, safe, but I still get nicks and scratches with a Mach3. For best results, shave every other day with it. At that point, you get a nice, close shave with little irritation. It gets a bit painful for everyday use, though. If you can swing the extra dough, I'd recommend the Mach3 Turbo instead.

review_text: I bought these for my Mom, who is 94. She was wanting socks with tread on them. She loves these socks and said she feels safe from falling. I started off with one pair and ordered five more, when she said she loves them.
product_title: Double Sided Slipper Socks Pair (Large)
product_description:
Double sided, anti slip, slipper socks keep your feet warm and dry while providing a non-skid, slip resistant sole to help prevent accidental falls and slips. The anti-slip traction on these slipper, hospital type socks provides enough traction to keep you from sliding on floors. Double sided slipper socks are made of a lightweight, soft cotton material for maximum warmth and comfort. Stretchable material fits most feet even swollen or enlarged feet. Hospital type, anti-slip socks are great for kids, hospital patients, post surgical patients and elderly retirement homes residents. Latex free slipper socks. Sold per pair. Select size when ordering. Medium fits children shoe sizes 7-11, Women's Shoe Sizes 6-7 and Mens Shoe Size 5-6, total length 9"
Large fits Womens shoe sizes 8-10 and Mens shoe sizes 7-9, total length 10 1/2"
Xlarge fits Womens Shoe Size 11-13 and Mens shoe sizes 10-12, total length 13 1/2"
XXlarge fits Mens shoe sizes 12-13, total length 15"

review_summary: Good deal Very sharp and consistent. Can't beat the price BUY BULK once you've decided on a blade.
review_text: Before buying bulk use a place like tryablade.com. They have a set of 11 different Artist (this style) blades from different vendors at around $15. Then you might narrow it down and buy 20 of each. Once your done you'll know what you need and then you save a lot buying bulk like this. Good price as most of the Razor shops rob you charging $14-$19 for 20. This package brings that price down to between $8 - $9 depending on the vendor. I've noticed all of these deals come from Japan. Even Kai Milds which never seem to sell for less then 18 can be purchased from some vendors in bulk like this a a great price. I have a SE collection and use Feathers on most of my razors and the Kai Milds on the ones that like to bite. These are very sharp. Even sharper then the DE Feathers although the SE Pros seem to be more consistent. I've tried the Pro supers but they're killers as they stick out more. Some swear by them, I just swear at them.

review_summary: Great blades- but not for sensative skin
review_text: I am a barber and use these only for razor fades, or face shaves. They glide effortlessly across the skin, and get an INCREDIBLY close shave. That being said, if you have very sensative skin, this blade WILL be too aggressive for your skin. I would recommend their soft razor in the light blue casing. It only comes in a box of 15 but its better than tearing up your skin. Personna is a good brand, as well, but because FEATHER has a special setting for their blades, it unfortunately won't work with their razors. If you have a feather razor, these, or the soft blades are 10/10. If you have a different razor that fits regular blades, i recommend using their yellow case FEAHER razor (again, may be too aggressive if you have sensative skin), Personna, or Dorco. I bought the 200 pack because for the price it was such a good deal, as well as since i am a barber i wanted more than just 3 or 5 packs before i had to order more. Will definitelt be buying this again

review_summary: There are 200 State of The Art Feather Professional Straight Razor Blade in These 10 Easy to Extract & Load Cartridges. Enjoy!
review_text: I use these Feather Profession Blade Artist Club blades in my Feather Artist Club SS Scotch Wood Folding Straight Razor. My thought is that is you are going to use a straight razor to shave or trim your facial hair or around the borders of your ears and neckline, use the safest and most reliable tools used by expert barbers. Good straight razor blades are expensive; however, buying in bulk brings that price down considerably. Accordingly, I bought this box of 10 cartridges, each containing 20 double edged blades. The cartridge has a small protruding poll that connects to a port on the straight razor and a pull and push slide that move the blade from the cartridge to the razor. Before inserting the new blade into the straight razor pinch the cloths pin style receiver on the razor. Then slide the blade extractor moving the blade into the receiver slot of the straight before you stop pinching to close the blade firmly in place. Additionally, the bottom of the cartridge has a convenient slot to safely store used blades. These blades and cartridges may seem expensive; however, I use each blade at least 20 times. Accordingly, the 200 blades in these 10 cartridges will last me over a decade or about 2.5 cents per day. That is a real bargain compared to the cost of each professional shave. Accordingly, for those of us who have the time and patience to learn how to safely use a straight razor, I whole heartedly recommend this superb, yet inexpensive product. Enjoy!
